channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Japan Overhauls Defense Export Rules, Opens Door to Global Arms Market
item.description: Japan scrapped decades-old restrictions on weapons exports, now allowing warships, missiles and fighter jets to be sold abroad. The major policy shift marks a departure from Japan's postwar pacifist constitution amid rising regional tensions.

item.title: New Zealand PM Luxon Survives Leadership Confidence Vote
item.description: Prime Minister Christopher Luxon retained the backing of his National Party lawmakers after calling a confidence vote amid sliding poll numbers months before the general election. He left without taking questions from reporters.

item.title: Gulf States Fear Iran Could Cement Control of Hormuz Strait
item.description: Gulf nations worry that US-Iran peace talks may only secure limited outcomes, potentially leaving Tehran with lasting leverage over the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Former Russian President Medvedev's warning crystallised regional fears.

item.title: British Royals Mark Queen Elizabeth's 100th Birthday
item.description: King Charles visited an exhibition dedicated to his late mother's fashion on what would have been Queen Elizabeth II's 100th birthday. The monarch would have turned 100 on Tuesday, with several royal events planned to mark the occasion.

item.title: Developing Nations Seek Path Out of Permacrisis at IMF Meetings
item.description: Policymakers from developing countries left IMF-World Bank meetings frustrated that successive external shocks are derailing efforts to tackle debt and deliver better lives for citizens struggling with food and fuel costs.

item.title: Vance Has Not Yet Departed for Pakistan Iran Talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance remains in the US as the deadline for Iran ceasefire talks approaches. Uncertainty surrounds whether negotiations will proceed before the truce expires Wednesday.

item.title: US-Philippines-Japan Start Military Drills Near Taiwan
item.description: Philippine and US forces launched annual exercises with expanded Japanese participation, conducting maritime strike drills on a remote island near Taiwan. Manila says the drills test "real-world" readiness.

item.title: China Warns US-Philippines-Japan Drills Risk Regional Trust
item.description: Beijing warned that expanded military cooperation between the US, Philippines, and Japan could undermine trust and deepen division in the region as the annual exercises began.

item.title: European Stocks Fall on US-Iran Ceasefire Concerns
item.description: European shares declined Monday as investors awaited progress on possible US-Iran talks ahead of the ceasefire expiry. Markets reassessed the durability of the two-week truce.

item.title: Board of Peace Envoy: Gaza Hamas Talks Not Easy
item.description: The Board of Peace lead envoy for Gaza said he was "fairly optimistic" about a Hamas disarmament plan but cautioned talks will take time. Negotiations continue amid broader regional tensions.

item.title: Zelensky criticizes US envoys for skipping Kyiv en route to Moscow
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelensky expressed frustration that US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are expected to meet with Ukrainian officials without visiting Kyiv first, calling it "disrespectful to come to Moscow and not Kyiv" as peace negotiations with Russia remain stalled.

item.title: China deploys warships for Pacific combat drills amid Japan tensions
item.description: A Chinese naval task group led by a warship will sail through the Bashi Channel into the Western Pacific for combat drills, as the US, Philippines and Japan conduct major joint exercises nearby in a show of allied military cooperation.

item.title: NATO fuel network strains European commercial aviation
item.description: Airlines warn flights could be disrupted within weeks as Europe fuel system strains under competing military and commercial demand, with NATO priority fuel movements squeezing civilian jet fuel supplies.

item.title: Europe convenes parallel Gaza summits as Board of Peace stalls
item.description: European nations hosted two parallel meetings on Gaza reconstruction, seeking new avenues to work with the US as disarmament talks with Hamas make glacial progress and the strip faces an estimated $71 billion in reconstruction needs.

item.title: Trump labor secretary resigns amid misconduct investigation
item.description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned after an internal watchdog probe into misconduct allegations, including an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job. She becomes the third cabinet official to leave the Trump administration in recent months.

item.title: Israeli Strikes Kill Five in Gaza as Hamas Clashes with Militia
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least five Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas fighters clashed with an Israeli-backed militia. The violence highlights the complex multi-front tensions in the enclave.

item.title: European Defence Stocks Cool as Investors Reassess War Winners
item.description: Investors have cooled on European defence stocks as profit-taking and stretched valuations collide with uncertainty over the future of warfare, with the Iran conflict highlighting the effectiveness of low-cost drones.

item.title: Putins High-Tech Submarines Challenge NATO in Atlantic
item.description: Russias advanced submarines are increasingly challenging NATO in the North Atlantic, with RAF Lossiemouth serving as the front line of defense against Russian incursions from the Arctic.

item.title: Israeli Soldier Seen Striking Jesus Statue in Lebanon
item.description: An Israeli soldier was photographed striking a statue of Jesus in Lebanon, sparking condemnation. Prime Minister Netanyahu said he was stunned and saddened by the incident.

item.title: Taiwan Business Group Urges Politics-Free Trade with China
item.description: The head of a top Taiwan business group called for keeping politics out of trade and tourism exchanges with Beijing, after China unveiled new incentives for the island.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strike kills one at Russian Black Sea port
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Tuapse port sparked a fire and killed at least one person, marking the second attack on the facility in less than a week as Ukraine continues targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: China's Xi urges open Strait of Hormuz in call with Saudi crown prince
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for maintaining normal ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz in a phone call with Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman as Beijing steps up diplomatic efforts to end the Iran conflict.

item.title: Australian bank flags $503 million impairment hit from Middle East volatility
item.description: National Australia Bank expects credit impairment charges to double as the Iran war roils global financial markets, highlighting the economic fallout spreading beyond the Middle East.

item.title: Six women win 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize
item.description: The first all-women cohort of Goldman Prize winners hails from Colombia, Nigeria, Papua New Guinea, South Korea, the UK and the US, recognizing grassroots environmental activism.

item.title: Trump says he's not under pressure over Iran war deal as peace talks remain uncertain
item.description: Iran has balked at President Trump's demands for a peace deal amid the ongoing Strait of Hormuz standoff, with the ceasefire deadline approaching and uncertainty about whether Tehran will join talks in Pakistan.
